title: Rajas con Crema Roasted Pepper Cream

seo.title: Rajas con Crema: Authentic Mexican Roasted Poblano Cream
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make authentic Rajas con Crema, a creamy Mexican roasted poblano pepper dish. This vegetarian recipe is easy to follow and perfect for any meal!
longTailKeywords: how to make Rajas con Crema
Rajas con Crema with poblano peppers
lowCompetitionKeywords: what to serve with rajas con crema
seo.h1: Authentic Rajas con Crema: Mexican Roasted Poblano Cream Recipe
h2Headings: How to Make Rajas con Crema: Step-by-Step Instructions
Tips for Perfect Rajas con Crema
Serving Suggestions for Rajas con Crema
seo.imageAlt: A bowl of creamy Rajas con Crema with roasted poblano peppers, ready to serve as a side or main dish.
seo.imageCaption: Enjoy this rich and flavorful Rajas con Crema, a classic Mexican vegetarian dish featuring tender roasted poblano peppers in a creamy sauce.

description: Rajas con Crema is a beloved Mexican vegetarian dish featuring tender strips of roasted poblano peppers simmered in a rich, creamy sauce. This comforting and flavorful preparation offers a delightful balance of smoky heat and savory dairy, making it a perfect side or light main course. It's a true taste of home, simple yet incredibly satisfying.

ingredients.notes: roasted, peeled, deseeded, and cut into strips

instructions.title: Roast the Poblano Peppers
instructions.text: Preheat your broiler or a comal/griddle to high heat. Place the poblano peppers directly on the hot surface or under the broiler, turning frequently, until the skins are blistered and charred all over. This will take about 10-15 minutes.

instructions.title: Peel and Prepare Peppers
instructions.text: Once roasted, immediately transfer the hot peppers to a bowl and cover tightly with plastic wrap or place them in a sealed plastic bag. Let them steam for 10-15 minutes. This helps loosen the skin. Carefully peel off the charred skin, remove the stems and seeds, then slice the peppers into thin strips (rajas).

instructions.title: Saute Aromatics
instructions.text: In a large skillet over medium heat, add the vegetable oil. Once hot, add the thinly sliced white onion and cook until softened and translucent, about 5-7 minutes. Add the minced garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant, being careful not to burn it.

instructions.title: Combine with Peppers and Corn
instructions.text: Add the prepared poblano strips (rajas) and the corn kernels to the skillet. Stir gently to combine all the ingredients and cook for 3-5 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld.

instructions.title: Add the Cream Sauce
instructions.text: Pour in the crema Mexicana and milk. If using, stir in the chicken bouillon powder. Bring the mixture to a gentle simmer, stirring occasionally.

instructions.title: Simmer and Season
instructions.text: Reduce the heat to low and let the rajas con crema simmer for 5-10 minutes, allowing the sauce to thicken slightly and the flavors to fully develop. Season generously with salt and black pepper to taste.

instructions.title: Serve Warm
instructions.text: Remove from heat. Serve your Rajas con Crema warm, garnished with crumbled queso fresco if desired. It pairs wonderfully with warm tortillas, rice, or as a side dish.

tips: For the best smoky flavor, make sure to get a good char on your poblano peppers during roasting. Don't rush this step, as it's key to the dish's depth.

Authentic Rajas con Crema uses Crema Mexicana, which is thinner and less tangy than sour cream. If you can't find it, a mix of heavy cream and a touch of milk works well, or use sour cream thinned with a little milk.

Rajas con Crema is incredibly versatile. Serve it as a side dish with tacos or grilled meats, fold it into quesadillas, or enjoy it as a vegetarian main course with warm corn tortillas and a side of Mexican rice.
article: Rajas con Crema Roasted Pepper Cream is a beloved Mexican Vegetarian dish enjoyed across Mexican. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
